Question: What are the primary business pain points driving the Intelligent Solution Builder initiative?
Guidance: Describe top 3-5 issues with the current manual artifact process (e.g. inconsistency, speed, capacity constraints)
Notes: Understanding business drivers helps align the technical solution with business outcomes and prioritise features

Question: What does success look like at the end of Phase 1 (Pre-Sales MVP)?
Guidance: Reference measurable targets such as artifact turnaround time (target <2 business days) and first-pass QA pass rate (target ≥90%)
Notes: Critical for defining scope and measuring whether the MVP meets stakeholder expectations

Question: What are the success criteria for the overall platform at General Availability?
Guidance: Include throughput targets (e.g. ~24 active engagements per quarter) and consulting-hour reduction (target 40%)
Notes: Shapes the backlog prioritisation and go/no-go criteria for each phase

Question: Are the phase deadlines — Phase 1 by 2026-09-30, Phase 2 by 2026-12-15, GA in Q1 2027 — fixed or flexible?
Guidance: Identify which milestones are hard constraints vs aspirational targets
Notes: Directly impacts sprint planning, team size, and risk decisions

Question: What is the significance of the 2027-01-31 flagship client renewal deadline?
Guidance: Clarify the dependency: does the platform need to be live and generating deliverables before that date, or just available?
Notes: This is a hard external deadline; late delivery may have contractual or revenue implications

Question: Is the $300k–$450k budget allocated across all three phases, or specific to Phase 1?
Guidance: Clarify CapEx vs OpEx split and whether the ~$4k–$6k/month run cost is included in Year 1 budget
Notes: Right-sizes each phase's scope and informs build vs buy decisions

Question: Who has budget approval authority, and what is the approval process for scope changes?
Guidance: Identify the CTO as executive sponsor; clarify thresholds and timelines for change orders
Notes: Prevents delays caused by unplanned procurement or approval cycles

Question: Which stakeholder groups will formally sign off on each phase deliverable?
Guidance: Map sign-off: CTO (Phase gates), VP Engineering (technical acceptance), Head of Solutions (artifact quality), Security Lead (compliance)
Notes: Defines the governance model and prevents scope-creep or rework late in each phase

Question: How many pre-sales consultants will be primary day-to-day users of the platform?
Guidance: Provide current headcount and expected growth over 12 months
Notes: Informs Cognito user pool sizing, usage-quota design, and UI/UX requirements

Question: How do downstream consumers (Delivery Consulting and Sales teams) currently receive generated artifacts?
Guidance: Describe current handoff: email, shared drive, portal, etc.
Notes: Determines whether the platform needs a delivery/notification mechanism beyond S3 storage

Question: Describe the legacy monolith currently running on a single EC2 instance that is to be retired.
Guidance: Include programming language, OS, any APIs it exposes, data it holds, and estimated retirement timeline
Notes: Identifies decommission dependencies and potential data-migration or integration tasks

Question: What Google Workspace integrations exist today that may need to connect to or be replaced by the new platform?
Guidance: Cover Docs, Sheets, Slides, Drive — identify if any are used as artifact templates or collaboration surfaces
Notes: Determines whether template conversion (Word/Excel/PowerPoint) requires Google export pipelines

Question: Does Amatra already have an AWS Organisation, Control Tower landing zone, or existing AWS accounts in us-west-2?
Guidance: Provide account structure, VPC topology, and any shared services (logging, security hub, etc.)
Notes: Determines greenfield build scope vs landing-zone integration effort

Question: Are there any existing AWS programme agreements (MAP credits, EDP, APN) that should be leveraged for this project?
Guidance: Include credit amounts, expiry dates, and eligible services
Notes: Can materially reduce infrastructure costs and inform service selection

Question: Which Amazon Bedrock Claude model versions are preferred for artifact generation (e.g. Claude 3 Haiku, Sonnet, Opus)?
Guidance: Consider cost vs quality trade-offs for different artifact types (discovery questionnaires vs detailed designs)
Notes: Model selection drives per-token costs and latency; may differ by artifact type

Question: What is the expected average prompt + completion token count per artifact generation job?
Guidance: Estimate tokens per job and expected job volume per day/month
Notes: Critical input for Bedrock cost modelling and Lambda timeout/memory sizing

Question: Are there requirements to fine-tune or customise Bedrock models using Amatra's proprietary templates or past engagement data?
Guidance: Specify if custom model customisation (continued pre-training or fine-tuning) is in scope
Notes: Custom model training has significant additional cost and timeline implications

Question: How should long-running generation jobs (30–60 min) be orchestrated — Lambda with Step Functions, ECS Fargate, or another approach?
Guidance: Clarify whether Lambda's 15-min maximum execution limit has been accounted for; Step Functions may be required
Notes: Lambda alone cannot run 30–60 min jobs; orchestration architecture must be confirmed early

Question: What is the expected peak concurrency of simultaneous artifact generation jobs?
Guidance: Estimate maximum parallel jobs during peak usage periods (e.g. end-of-quarter proposal rush)
Notes: Determines Lambda concurrency limits, DynamoDB capacity mode, and Bedrock throughput quotas

Question: What REST API authentication mechanism will API Gateway use beyond Cognito JWT validation?
Guidance: Clarify whether API keys, IAM SigV4, Lambda authorisers, or WAF integration are required
Notes: Drives API Gateway configuration and security posture

Question: Which systems or tools will consume the platform API (e.g. internal portal, Salesforce CRM, Slack bot)?
Guidance: List all integration points and their data exchange patterns (sync vs async, webhooks, polling)
Notes: Identifies integration complexity and potential need for event-driven notification (SNS/SQS/EventBridge)

Question: What artifact types must the platform generate in Phase 1, and what are their output formats?
Guidance: e.g. discovery questionnaire (CSV/XLSX), solution briefing (PPTX), statement of work (DOCX), infrastructure costs (XLSX)
Notes: Determines the document-generation pipeline and S3 object structure

Question: How many client engagements are expected per month, and how long must artifacts be retained in S3?
Guidance: Include active retention and archive requirements (e.g. 7-year retention for audit purposes)
Notes: Drives S3 storage tier selection (Standard vs Intelligent-Tiering vs Glacier) and cost projection

Question: What is the expected size of input briefs and generated artifacts per engagement?
Guidance: Estimate average bytes/KB per input and per output artifact
Notes: Informs S3 PUT/GET cost estimates and DynamoDB item size planning

Question: How many Cognito user groups are required beyond 'admins'?
Guidance: e.g. pre-sales-consultants, read-only reviewers, delivery team — describe permissions per group
Notes: Determines Cognito user pool and identity pool configuration complexity

Question: What is the plan to migrate existing Okta user identities to Amazon Cognito?
Guidance: Clarify number of users, attributes to migrate, and whether Okta remains as a SAML/OIDC IdP federated into Cognito
Notes: Identity migration is a hard dependency for user acceptance testing; may require phased cut-over

Question: What are the acceptable response-time SLAs for synchronous API calls vs asynchronous job status polling?
Guidance: e.g. API response <500ms, job completion notification within 5 min of finishing
Notes: Drives API Gateway timeout settings, Lambda memory, and DynamoDB read/write capacity

Question: What is the target platform availability SLA (stated as 99.9%), and how is that measured?
Guidance: Clarify measurement window (monthly), exclusions, and whether planned maintenance counts as downtime
Notes: 99.9% equates to ~43.8 min/month downtime; drives multi-AZ and failover architecture decisions

Question: What existing Word, Excel, and PowerPoint artifact templates must be ingested into the automated pipeline?
Guidance: Provide a full inventory with version history and owner team for each template
Notes: Template ingestion is a Phase 1 dependency; complexity affects effort and timeline estimates

Question: How are per-user and global monthly usage limits defined (e.g. max jobs per user per month, max tokens per month)?
Guidance: Specify enforcement mechanism: hard block vs soft warning, and the admin interface for adjusting limits
Notes: Usage controls affect DynamoDB schema design and Lambda authoriser logic

Question: Confirm that SOC 2 Type II is required. Are there additional frameworks — GDPR, ISO 27001, or others — that apply?
Guidance: List all applicable compliance frameworks and any certifications Amatra must hold or inherit from AWS
Notes: Each framework adds specific controls; SOC 2 + GDPR together affect logging, data retention, and DPA agreements

Question: What data classification levels apply to client brief inputs and generated artifacts?
Guidance: e.g. Confidential/Internal — do any artifacts contain PII, financial data, or trade secrets?
Notes: Determines encryption tier, S3 bucket policies, and DLP requirements

Question: Confirm that all customer data must remain in the United States (us-west-2). Is a second US region required for DR?
Guidance: Identify if us-east-1 or another US region is acceptable for disaster recovery replication
Notes: Data-residency constraint rules out non-US Bedrock regions and affects DR architecture

Question: Will Amazon Cognito be the sole IdP, or will Okta remain federated via SAML/OIDC post-migration?
Guidance: Describe target state identity architecture and any phased federation approach during migration
Notes: Dual-IdP configurations add complexity and must be designed carefully to avoid token-trust issues

Question: Is Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) mandatory for all users, or only for the admins group?
Guidance: Specify MFA methods: TOTP, SMS, push notification
Notes: MFA enforcement is a SOC 2 control requirement; configuration must be set at Cognito user pool level

Question: What key management approach is required for S3 and DynamoDB encryption at rest?
Guidance: AWS-managed keys (SSE-S3/SSE-KMS with AWS-managed CMK) vs customer-managed CMK in AWS KMS
Notes: Customer-managed CMKs add operational overhead (key rotation, policies) but are often required for SOC 2 evidence

Question: Is TLS 1.2 minimum sufficient for data in transit, or is TLS 1.3 required?
Guidance: Applies to API Gateway, Bedrock, and all internal service-to-service calls
Notes: TLS version requirements may be dictated by client contracts or SOC 2 auditors

Question: What logging and audit trail requirements exist for artifact generation events?
Guidance: Specify retention period for CloudTrail, API Gateway access logs, and Lambda logs in CloudWatch
Notes: SOC 2 requires immutable audit logs; retention period (typically ≥1 year) must be defined upfront

Question: Is the platform entirely public-internet-facing via API Gateway, or are VPC-private endpoints required?
Guidance: Clarify if Lambda and DynamoDB should use VPC endpoints, and whether AWS WAF is required on API Gateway
Notes: WAF + VPC endpoints add cost and complexity but are often required for SOC 2 network controls

Question: What is the process for patching Lambda runtimes and third-party dependencies (e.g. Python packages)?
Guidance: Describe cadence, responsibility (Amatra Engineering), and tooling (Amazon Inspector, Dependabot, etc.)
Notes: Vulnerability management programme is a SOC 2 CC7 control requirement

Question: How must client brief data be handled after an engagement is closed or a client account is deleted?
Guidance: Specify data-deletion SLAs, right-to-erasure (GDPR Article 17) requirements, and S3 Object Lifecycle policies
Notes: GDPR data-subject deletion rights require automated lifecycle policies and documented procedures

Question: What is the Recovery Time Objective (RTO) for the platform in the event of a service outage?
Guidance: e.g. <1 hour, <4 hours, <24 hours — is 99.9% availability SLA consistent with a <1-hour RTO?
Notes: RTO drives multi-AZ Lambda deployment, DynamoDB global tables consideration, and failover automation design

Question: What is the Recovery Point Objective (RPO) — how much data loss is acceptable in a failure scenario?
Guidance: e.g. zero data loss, <5 minutes, <1 hour — DynamoDB point-in-time recovery and S3 versioning must align
Notes: RPO determines DynamoDB PITR configuration and S3 versioning/replication settings

Question: What monitoring and alerting stack will be used — Amazon CloudWatch native, Datadog, Grafana, or other?
Guidance: Include dashboards, alert channels (PagerDuty, Slack, email), and on-call rotation ownership
Notes: Monitoring architecture must be defined in Phase 1; post-launch visibility is critical for a 99.9% SLA

Question: What business-level metrics must be tracked in addition to infrastructure metrics?
Guidance: e.g. artifacts generated per day, job success/failure rate, average generation time, token consumption
Notes: Business KPIs are success-criteria metrics; CloudWatch custom metrics or a BI dashboard may be needed

Question: What support coverage is required for the platform — business hours (8×5), extended, or 24×7?
Guidance: Identify who provides L1/L2 support: internal Amatra Engineering or a managed-services partner
Notes: Support model affects staffing costs and must align with the 99.9% availability commitment

Question: Are there defined maintenance windows when planned downtime is acceptable?
Guidance: e.g. Sunday 00:00–04:00 Pacific — serverless architecture minimises downtime but DynamoDB index changes may require it
Notes: Even serverless deployments need maintenance windows for schema migrations and major Bedrock model version updates

Question: How will token-consumption quotas be managed as usage scales from MVP to GA?
Guidance: Describe Bedrock service-quota increase process and any reserved throughput (Provisioned Throughput) plans
Notes: Bedrock on-demand throughput has soft limits; provisioned throughput may be needed for peak SLA compliance

Question: What is the incident management and escalation process once the platform is live?
Guidance: Define severity levels, response times, escalation path (e.g. on-call engineer → VP Engineering → CTO)
Notes: Formalising this in Phase 1 prevents ad-hoc incident handling and is required for SOC 2 CC7

Question: What is the preferred approach for migrating from manual Google Workspace processes to the new platform?
Guidance: Options: phased rollout (pilot team first), department-wide cutover, or parallel run with manual as fallback
Notes: Migration approach determines change-management effort and the acceptable risk level for early adopters

Question: How will existing Okta user accounts be migrated to Amazon Cognito — bulk import, SCIM sync, or manual?
Guidance: Provide number of users to migrate and whether their session history or preferences must be preserved
Notes: Identity migration is a Phase 1 prerequisite; timeline must account for Okta licence run-down and Cognito testing

Question: Will the legacy EC2 monolith be decommissioned before or after Phase 1 go-live?
Guidance: Clarify any data or functionality that still depends on the monolith and must be replicated in the new platform
Notes: Parallel operation of legacy and new platform adds infrastructure cost and integration complexity

Question: What is the detailed scope boundary between Phase 1 (Pre-Sales MVP) and Phase 2 (Delivery + Terraform)?
Guidance: List which artifact types are in scope for each phase and which features (e.g. Terraform generation) are Phase 2-only
Notes: Clear phase boundaries prevent scope creep and protect the 2026-09-30 Phase 1 deadline

Question: What is the maximum acceptable downtime during the production launch (go-live cutover) for Phase 1?
Guidance: e.g. zero downtime blue/green deployment, <30-minute maintenance window outside business hours
Notes: Serverless architecture supports zero-downtime deploys, but first-time Cognito and DynamoDB provisioning may require brief windows

Question: What internal engineering resources from Amatra's team are available to support the build?
Guidance: Identify team members, roles, estimated weekly hours, and any competing project commitments
Notes: Resource availability directly impacts sprint velocity, vendor dependency, and overall timeline feasibility

Question: Will Amatra use an external delivery partner (SI/MSP) for build, or is this an internal-only build?
Guidance: If a partner is involved, identify procurement timeline and contracting approach
Notes: External partner onboarding can add 4–8 weeks to the start; must be factored into Phase 1 timeline

Question: What training is required for pre-sales consultants to use the new platform effectively?
Guidance: Cover platform navigation, brief authoring best practices, artifact review and QA process
Notes: Training plan must be delivered before GA; train-the-trainer model may scale better than individual sessions

Question: What admin training is required for the team managing Cognito user groups and usage quotas?
Guidance: Identify who will be the platform administrator and whether runbooks/playbooks must be delivered
Notes: Admin enablement is a go-live dependency; undocumented admin procedures create SOC 2 control gaps

Question: What is the User Acceptance Testing approach — who conducts it, what scenarios must pass, and what is the exit criteria?
Guidance: Define UAT participants (pre-sales consultants, Head of Solutions), scenario count, and acceptable defect threshold
Notes: UAT exit criteria gate the Phase 1 go-live; unclear criteria lead to delayed sign-off

Question: How will artifact quality be evaluated during UAT — automated scoring, manual peer review, or a QA rubric?
Guidance: Reference the ≥90% first-pass QA target; define what constitutes a 'pass'
Notes: Quality gate methodology must be agreed before UAT begins to avoid subjective sign-off disputes

Question: What happens to the project if the 2026-09-30 Phase 1 deadline is missed?
Guidance: Identify contractual, commercial, or reputational consequences and acceptable contingency plans
Notes: Flagship client renewal is contingent on faster turnaround; delay risk has direct revenue implications

Question: How will the team handle Amazon Bedrock service limits (tokens per minute) during peak concurrent job runs?
Guidance: Describe mitigation: job queuing (SQS), exponential back-off, or pre-purchased Provisioned Throughput
Notes: Bedrock throttling is the most likely cause of SLA breach; mitigation must be designed in Phase 1

Question: Are there third-party dependencies (e.g. document-generation libraries for DOCX/PPTX/XLSX) that must be evaluated and licensed?
Guidance: List any open-source or commercial libraries under consideration and confirm licence compatibility
Notes: Third-party library vetting can delay Lambda packaging; must be resolved before architecture is finalised

Question: What organisational change-management constraints exist around retiring the manual process?
Guidance: Identify resistance from teams who rely on manual Google Workspace workflows and the plan to address it
Notes: Adoption risk is as significant as technical risk; change management must be a formal workstream

Question: What assumptions underpin the $300k–$450k budget estimate, and which are most likely to change?
Guidance: e.g. team composition, scope of AI model customisation, number of artifact types in Phase 1
Notes: Budget assumptions must be validated during discovery; unstated assumptions are the leading cause of overruns
